sections.model: AI and Monetization in Developer Tools
sections.prompt: Raise your hand if you’ve paid for an AI tool out of your own pocket.
sections.hint: Examples: Copilot, Cursor, Claude Pro, Gemini paid tiers.
no_hands: What would convince you to pay—better accuracy, faster responses, or integrations?
Do you think free tiers are enough for most Flutter work?
hands_up: Which paid tool gave you the most value?
Did it save enough time to justify the cost?
How do you explain tool costs to your team or clients?
follow_ups: Who has switched back from paid to free—why?
Do you see tool pricing shaping adoption?

sections.prompt: Raise your hand if AI has changed how your team collaborates.
sections.hint: Examples: pair programming, code reviews, task handoffs, design discussions.
no_hands: Why do you think AI hasn’t impacted your teamwork yet?
Which part of teamwork would you like AI to help with?
hands_up: How has AI changed code reviews or PRs?
Has AI reduced (or increased) back-and-forth in team discussions?
Any fun examples where AI helped unblock collaboration?
follow_ups: Has AI shifted who takes the lead on certain tasks?
Do you see AI more as an individual tool or a team tool?

sections.prompt: Raise your hand if you’ve used AI as a teacher or learning buddy.
sections.hint: Examples: learning Flutter APIs, Dart 3 features, or new libraries.
no_hands: What learning tasks do you still prefer books, docs, or human mentors for?
What would make AI a more trustworthy teacher?
hands_up: What’s the best example of AI teaching you a Flutter/Dart trick?
Did AI ever explain something better than official docs?
Any cases where AI misled you and wasted time?
follow_ups: Do you see AI more useful for juniors or seniors when learning?
Who has a favorite learning with AI workflow to share?

sections.model: AI and the Future of Work
sections.prompt: Raise your hand if you think AI will change what it means to be a Flutter developer in 5 years.
no_hands: Why do you think AI won’t change the role much?
What skills will always stay human-driven?
hands_up: How do you imagine your role evolving with AI assistants?
What new skills might become essential?
Do you think AI will open more opportunities, or reduce them?
follow_ups: Who sees AI creating new kinds of developer jobs?
What would you tell a new developer starting today?
